     7→
     8→import FirebaseCore
     9→import SwiftData
    10→import SwiftUI
    11→import Swinject
    12→
    13→@main
    14→struct FitTodayApp: App {
    15→    private let appContainer: AppContainer
    16→    // 💡 Learn: Com @Observable, use @State em vez de @StateObject
    17→    @State private var sessionStore: WorkoutSessionStore
    18→    @AppStorage(AppStorageKeys.hasSeenWelcome) private var hasSeenWelcome = false
    19→    @Environment(\.scenePhase) private var scenePhase
    20→
    21→    init() {
    22→        // Configure Firebase
    23→        FirebaseApp.configure()
    24→
    25→        // Bootstrap de segredos (apenas Debug) - popula Keychain a partir de Secrets.plist
    26→        KeychainBootstrap.runIfNeeded()
    27→
    28→        let container = AppContainer.build()
    29→        self.appContainer = container
    30→        sessionStore = WorkoutSessionStore(resolver: container.container)
    31→        
    32→        // Configurar aparência global para tema escuro
    33→        configureGlobalAppearance()
    34→
    35→        // Record first launch date (once)
    36→        if UserDefaults.standard.object(forKey: AppStorageKeys.firstLaunchDate) == nil {
    37→            UserDefaults.standard.set(Date(), forKey: AppStorageKeys.firstLaunchDate)
    38→        }
    39→
    40→        // Prune stale exercise image cache (background, no impact on cold start)
    41→        Task.detached(priority: .background) {
    42→            await ExerciseImageCache.shared.pruneOldCache()
    43→        }
    44→        
    45→        #if DEBUG
    46→        // Log de inicialização
    47→        print("═══════════════════════════════════════════")
    48→        print("[FitToday] 🚀 App inicializado")
    49→        print("[FitToday] Debug mode: ativo")
    50→        print("[FitToday] DebugEntitlementOverride enabled: \(DebugEntitlementOverride.shared.isEnabled)")
    51→        print("[FitToday] DebugEntitlementOverride isPro: \(DebugEntitlementOverride.shared.isPro)")
    52→        print("═══════════════════════════════════════════")
    53→        #endif
    54→    }
    55→
    56→    var body: some Scene {
    57→        WindowGroup {
    58→            Group {
    59→                if hasSeenWelcome {
    60→                    TabRootView()
    61→                } else {
    62→                    WelcomeOnboardingView()
    63→                }
    64→            }
    65→            // 💡 Learn: Com @Observable, use .environment() em vez de .environmentObject()
    66→            .environment(appContainer.router)
    67→            .environment(sessionStore)
    68→            .environment(\.dependencyResolver, appContainer.container)
    69→            .preferredColorScheme(.dark)  // Forçar tema escuro
    70→            .onOpenURL { url in
    71→                appContainer.router.handle(url: url)
    72→            }
    73→            .onAppear {
    74→                setupNetworkMonitor()
    75→            }
    76→        }
    77→        .modelContainer(appContainer.modelContainer)
    78→        .onChange(of: scenePhase) { _, newPhase in
    79→            if newPhase == .active {
    80→                Task {
    81→                    await processPendingQueue()
    82→                    await syncHealthKitWorkoutsIfAuthorized()
    83→                }
    84→            }
    85→        }
    86→    }
    87→
    88→    // MARK: - Offline Sync Queue Processing
    89→
    90→    /// Process pending sync queue when app becomes active or network is restored.
    91→    private func processPendingQueue() async {
    92→        guard let syncUseCase = appContainer.container.resolve(SyncWorkoutCompletionUseCase.self),
    93→              let queue = appContainer.container.resolve(PendingSyncQueue.self) else {
    94→            return
    95→        }
    96→
    97→        await queue.processQueue { entry in
    98→            try await syncUseCase.performSync(entry: entry)
    99→        }
   100→    }
   101→
   102→    /// Setup network monitor to process queue when connection is restored.
   103→    private func setupNetworkMonitor() {
   104→        guard let networkMonitor = appContainer.container.resolve(NetworkMonitor.self),
   105→              let syncUseCase = appContainer.container.resolve(SyncWorkoutCompletionUseCase.self),
   106→              let queue = appContainer.container.resolve(PendingSyncQueue.self) else {
   107→            return
   108→        }
   109→
   110→        networkMonitor.onConnectionRestored = {
   111→            await queue.processQueue { entry in
   112→                try await syncUseCase.performSync(entry: entry)
   113→            }
   114→        }
   115→
   116→        networkMonitor.startMonitoring()
   117→
   118→        #if DEBUG
   119→        print("[FitTodayApp] Network monitor configured for offline sync queue")
   120→        #endif
   121→    }
   122→    
   123→    // MARK: - HealthKit Auto-Sync
   124→
   125→    /// Syncs HealthKit workouts on app launch if authorized.
   126→    /// Imports external workouts and syncs existing app workouts.
   127→    private func syncHealthKitWorkoutsIfAuthorized() async {
   128→        guard let healthKitService = appContainer.container.resolve(HealthKitServicing.self),
   129→              let syncService = appContainer.container.resolve(HealthKitHistorySyncService.self) else {
   130→            #if DEBUG
   131→            print("[FitTodayApp] HealthKit services not available for auto-sync")
   132→            #endif
   133→            return
   134→        }
   135→
   136→        // Check if HealthKit is authorized
   137→        let authStatus = await healthKitService.authorizationState()
   138→        guard authStatus == .authorized else {
   139→            #if DEBUG
   140→            print("[FitTodayApp] HealthKit not authorized (status: \(authStatus)), skipping auto-sync")
   141→            #endif
   142→            return
   143→        }
   144→
   145→        #if DEBUG
   146→        print("[FitTodayApp] 🏃 Starting HealthKit auto-sync...")
   147→        #endif
   148→
   149→        do {
   150→            // Import external workouts from last 7 days
   151→            let importedCount = try await syncService.importExternalWorkouts(days: 7)
   152→            #if DEBUG
   153→            print("[FitTodayApp] ✅ Imported \(importedCount) external workouts from Apple Health")
   154→            #endif
   155→
   156→            // Sync existing app workouts with HealthKit data
   157→            let syncedCount = try await syncService.syncLastDays(7)
   158→            #if DEBUG
   159→            print("[FitTodayApp] ✅ Synced \(syncedCount) app workouts with Apple Health")
   160→            #endif
   161→        } catch {
   162→            #if DEBUG
   163→            print("[FitTodayApp] ❌ HealthKit sync failed: \(error.localizedDescription)")
   164→            #endif
   165→        }
   166→    }
   167→
   168→    /// Configura a aparência global de UIKit components para tema escuro
   169→    private func configureGlobalAppearance() {
   170→        // Tab Bar
   171→        let tabBarAppearance = UITabBarAppearance()
   172→        tabBarAppearance.configureWithOpaqueBackground()
   173→        tabBarAppearance.backgroundColor = UIColor(FitTodayColor.background)
   174→        
   175→        // Cores dos ícones
   176→        let normalColor = UIColor(FitTodayColor.textSecondary)
   177→        let selectedColor = UIColor(FitTodayColor.brandPrimary)
   178→        
   179→        tabBarAppearance.stackedLayoutAppearance.normal.iconColor = normalColor
   180→        tabBarAppearance.stackedLayoutAppearance.normal.titleTextAttributes = [.foregroundColor: normalColor]
   181→        tabBarAppearance.stackedLayoutAppearance.selected.iconColor = selectedColor
   182→        tabBarAppearance.stackedLayoutAppearance.selected.titleTextAttributes = [.foregroundColor: selectedColor]
   183→        
   184→        UITabBar.appearance().standardAppearance = tabBarAppearance
   185→        UITabBar.appearance().scrollEdgeAppearance = tabBarAppearance
   186→        
   187→        // Navigation Bar
   188→        let navBarAppearance = UINavigationBarAppearance()
   189→        navBarAppearance.configureWithOpaqueBackground()
   190→        navBarAppearance.backgroundColor = UIColor(FitTodayColor.background)
   191→        navBarAppearance.titleTextAttributes = [
   192→            .foregroundColor: UIColor.white,
   193→            .font: UIFont.systemFont(ofSize: 17, weight: .semibold)
   194→        ]
   195→        navBarAppearance.largeTitleTextAttributes = [
   196→            .foregroundColor: UIColor.white,
   197→            .font: UIFont.systemFont(ofSize: 34, weight: .bold)
   198→        ]
   199→        
   200→        UINavigationBar.appearance().standardAppearance = navBarAppearance
   201→        UINavigationBar.appearance().compactAppearance = navBarAppearance
   202→        UINavigationBar.appearance().scrollEdgeAppearance = navBarAppearance
   203→        UINavigationBar.appearance().tintColor = UIColor(FitTodayColor.brandPrimary)
   204→        
   205→        // Scroll View e TableView backgrounds
   206→        UITableView.appearance().backgroundColor = UIColor(FitTodayColor.background)
   207→        UICollectionView.appearance().backgroundColor = UIColor(FitTodayColor.background)
   208→    }
   209→}

1→//
     2→//  FirebaseAuthService.swift
     3→//  FitToday
     4→//
     5→//  Created by Claude on 16/01/26.
     6→//
     7→
     8→import AuthenticationServices
     9→import CryptoKit
    10→import FirebaseAuth
    11→import FirebaseFirestore
    12→import Foundation
    13→
    14→// MARK: - FirebaseAuthService
    15→
    16→actor FirebaseAuthService {
    17→    private let db = Firestore.firestore()
    18→    private var currentNonce: String?
    19→
    20→    // MARK: - ID Token
    21→
    22→    /// Returns the current user's Firebase ID token for authenticating with external APIs.
    23→    func getIDToken() async throws -> String {
    24→        guard let firebaseUser = Auth.auth().currentUser else {
    25→            throw AuthError.userNotFound
    26→        }
    27→        return try await firebaseUser.getIDToken()
    28→    }
    29→
    30→    // MARK: - Current User
    31→
    32→    func getCurrentUser() async throws -> SocialUser? {
    33→        guard let firebaseUser = Auth.auth().currentUser else {
    34→            return nil
    35→        }
    36→
    37→        return try await fetchUserFromFirestore(userId: firebaseUser.uid)
    38→    }
    39→
    40→    // MARK: - Apple Sign-In
    41→
    42→    func signInWithApple(credential: ASAuthorizationAppleIDCredential) async throws -> SocialUser {
    43→        guard let nonce = currentNonce else {
    44→            throw AuthError.appleSignInFailed(reason: "Invalid state: nonce not set")
    45→        }
    46→
    47→        guard let appleIDToken = credential.identityToken,
    48→              let idTokenString = String(data: appleIDToken, encoding: .utf8) else {
    49→            throw AuthError.appleSignInFailed(reason: "Unable to fetch identity token")
    50→        }
    51→
    52→        let oAuthCredential = OAuthProvider.appleCredential(
    53→            withIDToken: idTokenString,
    54→            rawNonce: nonce,
    55→            fullName: credential.fullName
    56→        )
    57→
    58→        let authResult = try await Auth.auth().signIn(with: oAuthCredential)
    59→        let firebaseUser = authResult.user
    60→
    61→        // Extract display name from credential or Firebase user
    62→        var displayName = "User"
    63→        if let fullName = credential.fullName {
    64→            let givenName = fullName.givenName ?? ""
    65→            let familyName = fullName.familyName ?? ""
    66→            let fullNameString = [givenName, familyName].filter { !$0.isEmpty }.joined(separator: " ")
    67→            if !fullNameString.isEmpty {
    68→                displayName = fullNameString
    69→            }
    70→        }
    71→        if displayName == "User", let firebaseDisplayName = firebaseUser.displayName, !firebaseDisplayName.isEmpty {
    72→            displayName = firebaseDisplayName
    73→        }
    74→
    75→        let socialUser = SocialUser(
    76→            id: firebaseUser.uid,
    77→            displayName: displayName,
    78→            email: firebaseUser.email ?? credential.email,
    79→            photoURL: firebaseUser.photoURL,
    80→            authProvider: .apple,
    81→            currentGroupId: nil,
    82→            privacySettings: PrivacySettings(shareWorkoutData: true),
    83→            createdAt: Date()
    84→        )
    85→
    86→        try await saveUserToFirestore(socialUser, isNewUser: authResult.additionalUserInfo?.isNewUser ?? true)
    87→        return try await fetchUserFromFirestore(userId: firebaseUser.uid) ?? socialUser
    88→    }
    89→
    90→    // MARK: - Google Sign-In
    91→
    92→    func signInWithGoogle(idToken: String, accessToken: String) async throws -> SocialUser {
    93→        let credential = GoogleAuthProvider.credential(
    94→            withIDToken: idToken,
    95→            accessToken: accessToken
    96→        )
    97→
    98→        let authResult = try await Auth.auth().signIn(with: credential)
    99→        let firebaseUser = authResult.user
   100→
   101→        let socialUser = SocialUser(
   102→            id: firebaseUser.uid,
   103→            displayName: firebaseUser.displayName ?? "User",
   104→            email: firebaseUser.email,
   105→            photoURL: firebaseUser.photoURL,
   106→            authProvider: .google,
   107→            currentGroupId: nil,
   108→            privacySettings: PrivacySettings(shareWorkoutData: true),
   109→            createdAt: Date()
   110→        )
   111→
   112→        try await saveUserToFirestore(socialUser, isNewUser: authResult.additionalUserInfo?.isNewUser ?? true)
   113→        return try await fetchUserFromFirestore(userId: firebaseUser.uid) ?? socialUser
   114→    }
   115→
   116→    // MARK: - Email/Password Sign-In
   117→
   118→    func signInWithEmail(_ email: String, password: String) async throws -> SocialUser {
   119→        let authResult: AuthDataResult
   120→        do {
   121→            authResult = try await Auth.auth().signIn(withEmail: email, password: password)
   122→        } catch {
   123→            throw AuthError.emailSignInFailed(reason: error.localizedDescription)
   124→        }
   125→
   126→        let firebaseUser = authResult.user
   127→
   128→        guard let user = try await fetchUserFromFirestore(userId: firebaseUser.uid) else {
   129→            throw AuthError.userNotFound
   130→        }
   131→
   132→        return user
   133→    }
   134→
   135→    // MARK: - Create Account
   136→
   137→    func createAccount(email: String, password: String, displayName: String) async throws -> SocialUser {
   138→        let authResult: AuthDataResult
   139→        do {
   140→            authResult = try await Auth.auth().createUser(withEmail: email, password: password)
   141→        } catch {
   142→            throw AuthError.emailSignInFailed(reason: error.localizedDescription)
   143→        }
   144→
   145→        let firebaseUser = authResult.user
   146→
   147→        // Update display name in Firebase Auth
   148→        let changeRequest = firebaseUser.createProfileChangeRequest()
   149→        changeRequest.displayName = displayName
   150→        try? await changeRequest.commitChanges()
   151→
   152→        let socialUser = SocialUser(
   153→            id: firebaseUser.uid,
   154→            displayName: displayName,
   155→            email: firebaseUser.email,
   156→            photoURL: nil,
   157→            authProvider: .email,
   158→            currentGroupId: nil,
   159→            privacySettings: PrivacySettings(shareWorkoutData: true),
   160→            createdAt: Date()
   161→        )
   162→
   163→        try await saveUserToFirestore(socialUser, isNewUser: true)
   164→        return socialUser
   165→    }
   166→
   167→    // MARK: - Sign Out
   168→
   169→    func signOut() throws {
   170→        try Auth.auth().signOut()
   171→    }
   172→
   173→    // MARK: - Auth State Observer
   174→
   175→    nonisolated func observeAuthState() -> AsyncStream<User?> {
   176→        AsyncStream { continuation in
   177→            let handle = Auth.auth().addStateDidChangeListener { _, user in
   178→                continuation.yield(user)
   179→            }
   180→
   181→            continuation.onTermination = { _ in
   182→                Auth.auth().removeStateDidChangeListener(handle)
   183→            }
   184→        }
   185→    }
   186→
   187→    // MARK: - Nonce Generation for Apple Sign-In
   188→
   189→    func prepareAppleSignIn() -> String {
   190→        let nonce = randomNonceString()
   191→        currentNonce = nonce
   192→        return sha256(nonce)
   193→    }
   194→
   195→    // MARK: - Private Helpers
   196→
   197→    private func saveUserToFirestore(_ user: SocialUser, isNewUser: Bool) async throws {
   198→        let userRef = db.collection("users").document(user.id)
   199→
   200→        if isNewUser {
   201→            try await userRef.setData(user.toDictionary())
   202→        } else {
   203→            // Only update non-critical fields for existing users
   204→            try await userRef.setData([
   205→                "displayName": user.displayName,
   206→                "email": user.email as Any,
   207→                "photoURL": user.photoURL?.absoluteString as Any
   208→            ], merge: true)
   209→        }
   210→    }
   211→
   212→    private func fetchUserFromFirestore(userId: String) async throws -> SocialUser? {
   213→        let document = try await db.collection("users").document(userId).getDocument()
   214→
   215→        guard document.exists else {
   216→            return nil
   217→        }
   218→
   219→        let fbUser = try document.data(as: FBUser.self)
   220→        return fbUser.toDomain()
   221→    }
   222→
   223→    // MARK: - Crypto Helpers
   224→
   225→    private func randomNonceString(length: Int = 32) -> String {
   226→        precondition(length > 0)
   227→        var randomBytes = [UInt8](repeating: 0, count: length)
   228→        let errorCode = SecRandomCopyBytes(kSecRandomDefault, randomBytes.count, &randomBytes)
   229→        if errorCode != errSecSuccess {
   230→            fatalError("Unable to generate nonce. SecRandomCopyBytes failed with OSStatus \(errorCode)")
   231→        }
   232→
   233→        let charset: [Character] = Array("REDACTED._")
   234→        let nonce = randomBytes.map { byte in
   235→            charset[Int(byte) % charset.count]
   236→        }
   237→
   238→        return String(nonce)
   239→    }
   240→
   241→    private func sha256(_ input: String) -> String {
   242→        let inputData = Data(input.utf8)
   243→        let hashedData = SHA256.hash(data: inputData)
   244→        let hashString = hashedData.compactMap {
   245→            String(format: "%02x", $0)
   246→        }.joined()
   247→
   248→        return hashString
   249→    }
   250→}
   251→

1→//
     2→//  FirebasePersonalTrainerService.swift
     3→//  FitToday
     4→//
     5→//  Created by Claude on 04/02/26.
     6→//
     7→
     8→import FirebaseFirestore
     9→import Foundation
    10→
    11→// MARK: - FirebasePersonalTrainerService
    12→
    13→/// Actor-based service for Firebase personal trainer operations.
    14→///
    15→/// Handles all Firestore interactions for personal trainers and
    16→/// trainer-student relationships.
    17→actor FirebasePersonalTrainerService {
    18→    private let db = Firestore.firestore()
    19→
    20→    // MARK: - Collection References
    21→
    22→    private var trainersCollection: CollectionReference {
    23→        db.collection("personalTrainers")
    24→    }
    25→
    26→    private var relationshipsCollection: CollectionReference {
    27→        db.collection("trainerStudents")
    28→    }
    29→
    30→    // MARK: - Fetch Trainer
    31→
    32→    /// Fetches a personal trainer by their ID.
    33→    ///
    34→    /// - Parameter id: The trainer's document ID.
    35→    /// - Returns: The Firebase trainer DTO.
    36→    /// - Throws: An error if the trainer is not found or fetch fails.
    37→    func fetchTrainer(id: String) async throws -> FBPersonalTrainer {
    38→        let snapshot = try await trainersCollection.document(id).getDocument()
    39→
    40→        guard snapshot.exists else {
    41→            throw NSError(
    42→                domain: "FirebasePersonalTrainerService",
    43→                code: 404,
    44→                userInfo: [NSLocalizedDescriptionKey: "Personal trainer not found"]
    45→            )
    46→        }
    47→
    48→        return try snapshot.data(as: FBPersonalTrainer.self)
    49→    }
    50→
    51→    // MARK: - Search Trainers
    52→
    53→    /// Searches for personal trainers by display name.
    54→    ///
    55→    /// - Parameters:
    56→    ///   - query: The search query string.
    57→    ///   - limit: Maximum number of results to return.
    58→    /// - Returns: An array of tuples containing document IDs and trainer DTOs.
    59→    func searchTrainers(query: String, limit: Int) async throws -> [(String, FBPersonalTrainer)] {
    60→        // Use Firestore's prefix query for display name search
    61→        let normalizedQuery = query.lowercased()
    62→        let endQuery = normalizedQuery + "\u{f8ff}"
    63→
    64→        let snapshot = try await trainersCollection
    65→            .whereField("isActive", isEqualTo: true)
    66→            .whereField("displayName", isGreaterThanOrEqualTo: normalizedQuery)
    67→            .whereField("displayName", isLessThanOrEqualTo: endQuery)
    68→            .limit(to: limit)
    69→            .getDocuments()
    70→
    71→        return try snapshot.documents.compactMap { doc -> (String, FBPersonalTrainer)? in
    72→            let trainer = try doc.data(as: FBPersonalTrainer.self)
    73→            return (doc.documentID, trainer)
    74→        }
    75→    }
    76→
    77→    // MARK: - Find by Invite Code
    78→
    79→    /// Finds a personal trainer by their unique invite code.
    80→    ///
    81→    /// - Parameter code: The invite code to search for.
    82→    /// - Returns: A tuple containing the document ID and trainer DTO, or nil if not found.
    83→    func findByInviteCode(_ code: String) async throws -> (String, FBPersonalTrainer)? {
    84→        let normalizedCode = code.uppercased().trimmingCharacters(in: .whitespacesAndNewlines)
    85→
    86→        let snapshot = try await trainersCollection
    87→            .whereField("inviteCode", isEqualTo: normalizedCode)
    88→            .whereField("isActive", isEqualTo: true)
    89→            .limit(to: 1)
    90→            .getDocuments()
    91→
    92→        guard let doc = snapshot.documents.first else {
    93→            return nil
    94→        }
    95→
    96→        let trainer = try doc.data(as: FBPersonalTrainer.self)
    97→        return (doc.documentID, trainer)
    98→    }
    99→
   100→    // MARK: - Request Connection
   101→
   102→    /// Creates a connection request from a student to a trainer.
   103→    ///
   104→    /// - Parameters:
   105→    ///   - trainerId: The trainer's document ID.
   106→    ///   - studentId: The student's user ID.
   107→    ///   - studentDisplayName: The student's display name (for notifications).
   108→    /// - Returns: The document ID of the created relationship.
   109→    /// - Throws: An error if a connection already exists with this trainer.
   110→    func requestConnection(
   111→        trainerId: String,
   112→        studentId: String,
   113→        studentDisplayName: String
   114→    ) async throws -> String {
   115→        // Check if relationship already exists
   116→        let existingRelationship = try await getCurrentRelationship(studentId: studentId)
   117→        if let existing = existingRelationship {
   118→            if existing.1.trainerId == trainerId && existing.1.status != TrainerConnectionStatus.cancelled.rawValue {
   119→                throw NSError(
   120→                    domain: "FirebasePersonalTrainerService",
   121→                    code: 409,
   122→                    userInfo: [NSLocalizedDescriptionKey: "Connection already exists with this trainer"]
   123→                )
   124→            }
   125→        }
   126→
   127→        // Create new relationship
   128→        let relationshipRef = relationshipsCollection.document()
   129→        let relationship = FBTrainerStudent(
   130→            id: relationshipRef.documentID,
   131→            trainerId: trainerId,
   132→            studentId: studentId,
   133→            status: TrainerConnectionStatus.pending.rawValue,
   134→            requestedBy: RequestedBy.student.rawValue,
   135→            requestedAt: nil, // ServerTimestamp will set this
   136→            acceptedAt: nil,
   137→            subscriptionStatus: TrainerSubscriptionStatus.trial.rawValue,
   138→            subscriptionExpiresAt: nil,
   139→            createdAt: nil
   140→        )
   141→
   142→        try relationshipRef.setData(from: relationship)
   143→
   144→        #if DEBUG
   145→        print("[PersonalTrainerService] Created connection request from \(studentDisplayName) to trainer \(trainerId)")
   146→        #endif
   147→
   148→        return relationshipRef.documentID
   149→    }
   150→
   151→    // MARK: - Cancel Connection
   152→
   153→    /// Cancels an existing trainer-student connection.
   154→    ///
   155→    /// - Parameter relationshipId: The document ID of the relationship to cancel.
   156→    func cancelConnection(relationshipId: String) async throws {
   157→        try await relationshipsCollection.document(relationshipId).updateData([
   158→            "status": TrainerConnectionStatus.cancelled.rawValue
   159→        ])
   160→
   161→        #if DEBUG
   162→        print("[PersonalTrainerService] Cancelled connection \(relationshipId)")
   163→        #endif
   164→    }
   165→
   166→    // MARK: - Observe Relationship
   167→
   168→    /// Observes changes to a student's trainer relationship in real-time.
   169→    ///
   170→    /// - Parameter studentId: The student's user ID.
   171→    /// - Returns: An async stream emitting relationship updates or nil.
   172→    func observeRelationship(studentId: String) -> AsyncStream<(String, FBTrainerStudent)?> {
   173→        AsyncStream { continuation in
   174→            let listener = relationshipsCollection
   175→                .whereField("studentId", isEqualTo: studentId)
   176→                .whereField("status", in: [
   177→                    TrainerConnectionStatus.pending.rawValue,
   178→                    TrainerConnectionStatus.active.rawValue,
   179→                    TrainerConnectionStatus.paused.rawValue
   180→                ])
   181→                .limit(to: 1)
   182→                .addSnapshotListener { snapshot, error in
   183→                    if let error = error {
   184→                        #if DEBUG
   185→                        print("[PersonalTrainerService] Observe error: \(error.localizedDescription)")
   186→                        #endif
   187→                        return
   188→                    }
   189→
   190→                    guard let snapshot = snapshot,
   191→                          let doc = snapshot.documents.first else {
   192→                        continuation.yield(nil)
   193→                        return
   194→                    }
   195→
   196→                    do {
   197→                        let relationship = try doc.data(as: FBTrainerStudent.self)
   198→                        continuation.yield((doc.documentID, relationship))
   199→                    } catch {
   200→                        #if DEBUG
   201→                        print("[PersonalTrainerService] Parse error: \(error.localizedDescription)")
   202→                        #endif
   203→                        continuation.yield(nil)
   204→                    }
   205→                }
   206→
   207→            continuation.onTermination = { _ in
   208→                listener.remove()
   209→            }
   210→        }
   211→    }
   212→
   213→    // MARK: - Get Current Relationship
   214→
   215→    /// Fetches the current active relationship for a student.
   216→    ///
   217→    /// - Parameter studentId: The student's user ID.
   218→    /// - Returns: A tuple containing the document ID and relationship DTO, or nil.
   219→    func getCurrentRelationship(studentId: String) async throws -> (String, FBTrainerStudent)? {
   220→        let snapshot = try await relationshipsCollection
   221→            .whereField("studentId", isEqualTo: studentId)
   222→            .whereField("status", in: [
   223→                TrainerConnectionStatus.pending.rawValue,
   224→                TrainerConnectionStatus.active.rawValue,
   225→                TrainerConnectionStatus.paused.rawValue
   226→            ])
   227→            .limit(to: 1)
   228→            .getDocuments()
   229→
   230→        guard let doc = snapshot.documents.first else {
   231→            return nil
   232→        }
   233→
   234→        let relationship = try doc.data(as: FBTrainerStudent.self)
   235→        return (doc.documentID, relationship)
   236→    }
   237→}

     2→//  FirebaseNotificationService.swift
     3→//  FitToday
     4→//
     5→//  Created by Claude on 17/01/26.
     6→//
     7→
     8→import FirebaseFirestore
     9→import Foundation
    10→
    11→// MARK: - FirebaseNotificationService
    12→
    13→actor FirebaseNotificationService {
    14→    private let db = Firestore.firestore()
    15→    private let collectionName = "notifications"
    16→
    17→    // MARK: - Fetch Notifications
    18→
    19→    /// Fetches notifications for a user, limited to the last 7 days.
    20→    func fetchNotifications(userId: String) async throws -> [FBNotification] {
    21→        let sevenDaysAgo = Calendar.current.date(byAdding: .day, value: -7, to: Date()) ?? Date()
    22→
    23→        let snapshot = try await db.collection(collectionName)
    24→            .whereField("userId", isEqualTo: userId)
    25→            .whereField("createdAt", isGreaterThan: Timestamp(date: sevenDaysAgo))
    26→            .order(by: "createdAt", descending: true)
    27→            .limit(to: 50)
    28→            .getDocuments()
    29→
    30→        return snapshot.documents.compactMap { doc in
    31→            try? doc.data(as: FBNotification.self)
    32→        }
    33→    }
    34→
    35→    // MARK: - Observe Notifications (Real-Time)
    36→
    37→    func observeNotifications(userId: String) -> AsyncStream<[FBNotification]> {
    38→        AsyncStream { continuation in
    39→            let sevenDaysAgo = Calendar.current.date(byAdding: .day, value: -7, to: Date()) ?? Date()
    40→
    41→            let listener = db.collection(collectionName)
    42→                .whereField("userId", isEqualTo: userId)
    43→                .whereField("createdAt", isGreaterThan: Timestamp(date: sevenDaysAgo))
    44→                .order(by: "createdAt", descending: true)
    45→                .limit(to: 50)
    46→                .addSnapshotListener { snapshot, error in
    47→                    guard let documents = snapshot?.documents else {
    48→                        return
    49→                    }
    50→
    51→                    let notifications = documents.compactMap { doc in
    52→                        try? doc.data(as: FBNotification.self)
    53→                    }
    54→
    55→                    continuation.yield(notifications)
    56→                }
    57→
    58→            continuation.onTermination = { _ in
    59→                listener.remove()
    60→            }
    61→        }
    62→    }
    63→
    64→    // MARK: - Mark as Read
    65→
    66→    func markAsRead(notificationId: String) async throws {
    67→        try await db.collection(collectionName)
    68→            .document(notificationId)
    69→            .updateData(["isRead": true])
    70→    }
    71→
    72→    // MARK: - Mark All as Read
    73→
    74→    func markAllAsRead(userId: String) async throws {
    75→        let snapshot = try await db.collection(collectionName)
    76→            .whereField("userId", isEqualTo: userId)
    77→            .whereField("isRead", isEqualTo: false)
    78→            .getDocuments()
    79→
    80→        guard !snapshot.documents.isEmpty else { return }
    81→
    82→        let batch = db.batch()
    83→        for doc in snapshot.documents {
    84→            batch.updateData(["isRead": true], forDocument: doc.reference)
    85→        }
    86→
    87→        try await batch.commit()
    88→    }
    89→
    90→    // MARK: - Create Notification
    91→
    92→    func createNotification(
    93→        userId: String,
    94→        groupId: String,
    95→        type: NotificationType,
    96→        message: String
    97→    ) async throws {
    98→        let notification = FBNotification(
    99→            id: nil,
   100→            userId: userId,
   101→            groupId: groupId,
   102→            type: type.rawValue,
   103→            message: message,
   104→            isRead: false,
   105→            createdAt: nil // ServerTimestamp
   106→        )
   107→
   108→        _ = try await db.collection(collectionName).addDocument(from: notification)
   109→    }
   110→
   111→    // MARK: - Get Unread Count
   112→
   113→    func getUnreadCount(userId: String) async throws -> Int {
   114→        let snapshot = try await db.collection(collectionName)
   115→            .whereField("userId", isEqualTo: userId)
   116→            .whereField("isRead", isEqualTo: false)
   117→            .count
   118→            .getAggregation(source: .server)
   119→
   120→        return Int(truncating: snapshot.count)
   121→    }
   122→}

     8→import Foundation
     9→import SwiftData
    10→import Swinject
    11→
    12→/// Responsável por montar e expor o container de dependências da aplicação.
    13→struct AppContainer {
    14→    let container: Container
    15→    let router: AppRouter
    16→    let modelContainer: ModelContainer
    17→
    18→    @MainActor
    19→    static func build() -> AppContainer {
    20→        let modelContainer = makeModelContainer()
    21→        return build(modelContainer: modelContainer)
    22→    }
    23→
    24→    @MainActor
    25→    static func build(modelContainer: ModelContainer) -> AppContainer {
    26→        let container = Container()
    27→        container.register(ModelContainer.self) { _ in modelContainer }
    28→            .inObjectScope(.container)
    29→
    30→        let router = AppRouter()
    31→        container.register(AppRouting.self) { _ in router }
    32→            .inObjectScope(.container)
    33→
    34→        // Repositórios
    35→        container.register(UserProfileRepository.self) { _ in
    36→            SwiftDataUserProfileRepository(modelContainer: modelContainer)
    37→        }.inObjectScope(.container)
    38→
    39→        container.register(WorkoutHistoryRepository.self) { _ in
    40→            SwiftDataWorkoutHistoryRepository(modelContainer: modelContainer)
    41→        }.inObjectScope(.container)
    42→
    43→        container.register(UserStatsRepository.self) { _ in
    44→            SwiftDataUserStatsRepository(modelContainer: modelContainer)
    45→        }.inObjectScope(.container)
    46→        
    47→        // Workout Composition Cache (F7)
    48→        container.register(WorkoutCompositionCacheRepository.self) { _ in
    49→            SwiftDataWorkoutCompositionCacheRepository(modelContainer: modelContainer)
    50→        }.inObjectScope(.container)
    51→
    52→        // WorkoutBlocksRepository é registrado após configurar exercícios (para permitir enriquecimento de mídia/instruções).
    53→
    54→        // StoreKit Service e EntitlementRepository
    55→        // Nota: StoreKitService é @MainActor, então precisa ser criado no main thread
    56→        let storeKitService = StoreKitService()
    57→        container.register(StoreKitService.self) { _ in storeKitService }
    58→            .inObjectScope(.container)
    59→        
    60→        let entitlementRepository = StoreKitEntitlementRepository(modelContainer: modelContainer, storeKitService: storeKitService)
    61→        container.register(EntitlementRepository.self) { _ in entitlementRepository }
    62→            .inObjectScope(.container)
    63→        
    64→        // Feature Gating Use Case
    65→        let aiUsageTracker = SimpleAIUsageTracker()
    66→        container.register(AIUsageTracking.self) { _ in aiUsageTracker }
    67→            .inObjectScope(.container)
    68→        
    69→        container.register(FeatureGating.self) { resolver in
    70→            FeatureGatingUseCase(
    71→                entitlementRepository: resolver.resolve(EntitlementRepository.self)!,
    72→                usageTracker: resolver.resolve(AIUsageTracking.self)
    73→            )
    74→        }.inObjectScope(.container)
    75→
    76→        // ========== REMOTE CONFIG & FEATURE FLAGS ==========
    77→
    78→        // Remote Config Service - wraps Firebase Remote Config
    79→        let remoteConfigService = RemoteConfigService()
    80→        container.register(RemoteConfigServicing.self) { _ in remoteConfigService }
    81→            .inObjectScope(.container)
    82→
    83→        // Feature Flag Repository - provides feature flag access with caching
    84→        container.register(FeatureFlagRepository.self) { resolver in
    85→            RemoteConfigFeatureFlagRepository(
    86→                remoteConfigService: resolver.resolve(RemoteConfigServicing.self)!
    87→            )
    88→        }
    89→        .inObjectScope(.container)
    90→
    91→        // Feature Flag Use Case - combines feature flags with entitlement
    92→        container.register(FeatureFlagChecking.self) { resolver in
    93→            FeatureFlagUseCase(
    94→                featureFlagRepository: resolver.resolve(FeatureFlagRepository.self)!,
    95→                featureGating: resolver.resolve(FeatureGating.self)!
    96→            )
    97→        }
    98→        .inObjectScope(.container)
    99→
   100→        // ========== END REMOTE CONFIG & FEATURE FLAGS ==========
   101→
   102→        // Firestore Exercise Service
   103→        let exerciseService = FirestoreExerciseService()
   104→        container.register(ExerciseServiceProtocol.self) { _ in exerciseService }
   105→            .inObjectScope(.container)
   106→
   107→        // Workout Blocks Repository
   108→        let blocksRepository = BundleWorkoutBlocksRepository()
   109→        container.register(WorkoutBlocksRepository.self) { _ in blocksRepository }
   110→            .inObjectScope(.container)
   111→
   112→        // Library Workouts Repository - using base bundle repository (images from Firebase Storage)
   113→        let libraryRepository = BundleLibraryWorkoutsRepository()
   114→        container.register(LibraryWorkoutsRepository.self) { _ in libraryRepository }
   115→            .inObjectScope(.container)
   116→
   117→        // ProgramRepository - carrega programas do bundle
   118→        let programRepository = BundleProgramRepository()
   119→        container.register(ProgramRepository.self) { _ in programRepository }
   120→            .inObjectScope(.container)
   121→
   122→        // ProgramWorkoutRepository - carrega exercícios do catálogo Firestore para programas
   123→        let programWorkoutRepository = FirestoreProgramWorkoutRepository(exerciseService: exerciseService)
   124→        container.register(ProgramWorkoutRepository.self) { _ in programWorkoutRepository }
   125→            .inObjectScope(.container)
   126→
   127→        // ProgramWorkoutCustomizationRepository - salva customizações do usuário (ordem, exclusões)
   128→        let customizationRepository = UserDefaultsProgramWorkoutCustomizationRepository()
   129→        container.register(ProgramWorkoutCustomizationRepositoryProtocol.self) { _ in customizationRepository }
   130→            .inObjectScope(.container)
   131→
   132→        // LoadProgramWorkoutsUseCase - carrega treinos de programa com exercícios do catálogo
   133→        container.register(LoadProgramWorkoutsUseCase.self) { resolver in
   134→            LoadProgramWorkoutsUseCase(
   135→                programRepository: resolver.resolve(ProgramRepository.self)!,
   136→                workoutRepository: resolver.resolve(ProgramWorkoutRepository.self)!
   137→            )
   138→        }
   139→        .inObjectScope(.container)
   140→
   141→        // Workout Composition - New simplified OpenAI stack
   142→        // Enhanced local composer with variation validation
   143→        let historyRepo = container.resolve(WorkoutHistoryRepository.self)!
   144→        let enhancedLocalComposer = EnhancedLocalWorkoutPlanComposer(
   145→            historyRepository: historyRepo
   146→        )
   147→        container.register(EnhancedLocalWorkoutPlanComposer.self) { _ in enhancedLocalComposer }
   148→            .inObjectScope(.container)
   149→
   150→        // WorkoutPlanComposing: Use OpenAI if available, otherwise enhanced local
   151→        container.register(WorkoutPlanComposing.self) { resolver in
   152→            let historyRepository = resolver.resolve(WorkoutHistoryRepository.self)!
   153→
   154→            // Try to create OpenAI composer if API key is configured
   155→            if let client = NewOpenAIClient.fromUserKey() {
   156→                return NewOpenAIWorkoutComposer(
   157→                    client: client,
   158→                    localFallback: enhancedLocalComposer,
   159→                    historyRepository: historyRepository
   160→                )
   161→            } else {
   162→                // No API key - use enhanced local composer
   163→                return enhancedLocalComposer
   164→            }
   165→        }
   166→        .inObjectScope(.container)
   167→        
   168→        // HealthKit (iPhone) - serviço de integração (PRO gating é feito na UI/fluxos)
   169→        let healthKitService = HealthKitService()
   170→        container.register(HealthKitServicing.self) { _ in healthKitService }
   171→            .inObjectScope(.container)
   172→        
   173→        // Note: HealthKitHistorySyncService is registered later after SyncWorkoutCompletionUseCase is available
   174→
   175→        // HealthKit Workout Sync Use Case - auto-exports workouts and imports calories
   176→        container.register(SyncWorkoutWithHealthKitUseCase.self) { resolver in
   177→            SyncWorkoutWithHealthKitUseCase(
   178→                healthKitService: resolver.resolve(HealthKitServicing.self)!,
   179→                historyRepository: resolver.resolve(WorkoutHistoryRepository.self)!
   180→            )
   181→        }
   182→        .inObjectScope(.container)
   183→
   184→        // Chat Repository (persistence)
   185→        container.register(ChatRepository.self) { _ in
   186→            SwiftDataChatRepository(modelContainer: modelContainer)
   187→        }.inObjectScope(.container)
   188→
   189→        // AI Chat Service (FitOrb) — resolved lazily inside AIChatViewModel.
   190→        // Not registered here because the OpenAI API key may not exist at launch.
   191→        // The ViewModel creates AIChatService on-demand when a key is available.
   192→
   193→        // Firebase Analytics Service
   194→        let analyticsService = FirebaseAnalyticsService()
   195→        container.register(AnalyticsTracking.self) { _ in analyticsService }
   196→            .inObjectScope(.container)
   197→
   198→        // Firebase Authentication
   199→        let firebaseAuthService = FirebaseAuthService()
   200→        container.register(FirebaseAuthService.self) { _ in firebaseAuthService }

   633→
   634→    private static func makeModelContainer() -> ModelContainer {
   635→        let schema = Schema([
   636→            SDUserProfile.self,
   637→            SDWorkoutHistoryEntry.self,
   638→            SDProEntitlementSnapshot.self,
   639→            SDCachedWorkout.self,
   640→            SDUserStats.self,
   641→            SDCustomWorkoutTemplate.self,
   642→            SDCustomWorkoutCompletion.self,
   643→            SDSavedRoutine.self,
   644→            SDChatMessage.self
   645→        ])
   646→        
   647→        // Configuração com migração automática leve
   648→        let configuration = ModelConfiguration(
   649→            schema: schema,
   650→            isStoredInMemoryOnly: false,
   651→            allowsSave: true
   652→        )
   653→        
   654→        do {
   655→            let container = try ModelContainer(for: schema, configurations: [configuration])
   656→            #if DEBUG
   657→            print("[SwiftData] ✅ ModelContainer criado com sucesso")
   658→            #endif
   659→            return container
   660→        } catch {
   661→            // Em caso de erro de migração, logar mas NÃO apagar os dados
   662→            // O usuário deve limpar manualmente se necessário
   663→            #if DEBUG
   664→            print("❌ [SwiftData] Erro ao criar container: \(error.localizedDescription)")
   665→            print("❌ [SwiftData] Se persistir, limpe os dados do app manualmente")
   666→            #endif
   667→            fatalError("Could not create ModelContainer: \(error)")
   668→        }
   669→    }
   670→}
   671→
   672→

     2→//  FirebaseUserService.swift
     3→//  FitToday
     4→//
     5→//  Created by Claude on 16/01/26.

     7→
     8→import FirebaseFirestore
     9→import Foundation
    10→
    11→// MARK: - FirebaseUserService
    12→
    13→actor FirebaseUserService {
    14→    private let db = Firestore.firestore()
    15→
    16→    // MARK: - Get User
    17→
    18→    func getUser(_ userId: String) async throws -> FBUser? {
    19→        let snapshot = try await db.collection("users").document(userId).getDocument()
    20→
    21→        guard snapshot.exists else {
    22→            return nil
    23→        }
    24→
    25→        return try snapshot.data(as: FBUser.self)
    26→    }
    27→
    28→    // MARK: - Update User
    29→
    30→    func updateUser(_ user: FBUser) async throws {
    31→        guard let userId = user.id else {
    32→            throw NSError(
    33→                domain: "FirebaseUserService",
    34→                code: 400,
    35→                userInfo: [NSLocalizedDescriptionKey: "User ID is required"]
    36→            )
    37→        }
    38→
    39→        try await db.collection("users").document(userId).setData(from: user, merge: true)
    40→    }
    41→
    42→    // MARK: - Update Privacy Settings
    43→
    44→    func updatePrivacySettings(_ userId: String, settings: FBPrivacySettings) async throws {
    45→        try await db.collection("users").document(userId).updateData([
    46→            "privacySettings": [
    47→                "shareWorkoutData": settings.shareWorkoutData
    48→            ]
    49→        ])
    50→    }
    51→
    52→    // MARK: - Update Current Group
    53→
    54→    func updateCurrentGroup(_ userId: String, groupId: String?) async throws {
    55→        if let groupId = groupId {
    56→            try await db.collection("users").document(userId).updateData([
    57→                "currentGroupId": groupId
    58→            ])
    59→        } else {
    60→            // Remove field if nil
    61→            try await db.collection("users").document(userId).updateData([
    62→                "currentGroupId": FieldValue.delete()
    63→            ])
    64→        }
    65→    }
    66→}
    67→

1→//
     2→//  FirebaseStorageService.swift
     3→//  FitToday
     4→//
     5→//  Created by Claude on 25/01/26.
     6→//
     7→
     8→import FirebaseStorage
     9→import Foundation
    10→
    11→// MARK: - StorageError
    12→
    13→/// Errors that can occur during storage operations.
    14→enum StorageError: Error, LocalizedError {
    15→    case uploadFailed(path: String, underlyingError: Error)
    16→    case deleteFailed(path: String, underlyingError: Error)
    17→    case invalidConfiguration
    18→
    19→    var errorDescription: String? {
    20→        switch self {
    21→        case .uploadFailed(let path, let error):
    22→            return "Falha ao enviar arquivo para '\(path)': \(error.localizedDescription)"
    23→        case .deleteFailed(let path, let error):
    24→            return "Falha ao deletar arquivo '\(path)': \(error.localizedDescription)"
    25→        case .invalidConfiguration:
    26→            return "Configuração do Firebase Storage inválida"
    27→        }
    28→    }
    29→}
    30→
    31→// MARK: - StorageServicing Protocol
    32→
    33→/// Protocol for storage operations.
    34→protocol StorageServicing: Sendable {
    35→    /// Uploads image data to storage at the specified path.
    36→    /// - Parameters:
    37→    ///   - data: The image data to upload (JPEG format expected)
    38→    ///   - path: The storage path where the image will be stored
    39→    /// - Returns: The download URL of the uploaded image
    40→    func uploadImage(data: Data, path: String) async throws -> URL
    41→
    42→    /// Deletes an image from storage at the specified path.
    43→    /// - Parameter path: The storage path of the image to delete
    44→    func deleteImage(path: String) async throws
    45→}
    46→
    47→// MARK: - FirebaseStorageService
    48→
    49→/// Actor-based Firebase Storage service for thread-safe image operations.
    50→actor FirebaseStorageService: StorageServicing {
    51→    private let storage = Storage.storage()
    52→
    53→    func uploadImage(data: Data, path: String) async throws -> URL {
    54→        let ref = storage.reference().child(path)
    55→
    56→        let metadata = StorageMetadata()
    57→        metadata.contentType = "image/jpeg"
    58→
    59→        #if DEBUG
    60→        print("[Storage] 📤 Uploading image to: \(path) (\(data.count / 1024) KB)")
    61→        print("[Storage] 📦 Storage bucket: \(storage.reference().bucket)")
    62→        #endif
    63→
    64→        do {
    65→            // Upload the data and wait for completion
    66→            let uploadResult = try await ref.putDataAsync(data, metadata: metadata)
    67→
    68→            #if DEBUG
    69→            print("[Storage] 📤 Upload metadata returned - path: \(uploadResult.path ?? "nil")")
    70→            #endif
    71→
    72→            // Get the download URL from the same reference we uploaded to
    73→            let url = try await ref.downloadURL()
    74→
    75→            #if DEBUG
    76→            print("[Storage] ✅ Upload successful: \(url.absoluteString.prefix(80))...")
    77→            #endif
    78→
    79→            return url
    80→        } catch let error as NSError {
    81→            #if DEBUG
    82→            print("[Storage] ❌ Upload failed: \(error.localizedDescription)")
    83→            print("[Storage] Error code: \(error.code)")
    84→            print("[Storage] Error domain: \(error.domain)")
    85→            print("[Storage] Full error: \(error)")
    86→
    87→            // Provide more helpful error messages based on error code
    88→            switch error.code {
    89→            case -13010: // Object does not exist
    90→                print("[Storage] ⚠️ Hint: This usually means Firebase Storage rules don't allow writes to this path.")
    91→                print("[Storage] ⚠️ Check your storage.rules file allows: 'write' to '/checkIns/{groupId}/{userId}/{filename}'")
    92→            case -13021: // Unauthenticated
    93→                print("[Storage] ⚠️ Hint: User is not authenticated. Ensure Firebase Auth is configured.")
    94→            case -13000: // Unknown error
    95→                print("[Storage] ⚠️ Hint: Check network connectivity and Firebase configuration.")
    96→            default:
    97→                break
    98→            }
    99→            #endif
   100→
   101→            throw StorageError.uploadFailed(
   102→                path: path,
   103→                underlyingError: error
   104→            )
   105→        } catch {
   106→            #if DEBUG
   107→            print("[Storage] ❌ Unexpected error: \(error)")
   108→            #endif
   109→            throw StorageError.uploadFailed(path: path, underlyingError: error)
   110→        }
   111→    }
   112→
   113→    func deleteImage(path: String) async throws {
   114→        let ref = storage.reference().child(path)
   115→        try await ref.delete()
   116→    }
   117→}

   127→        self.createdAt = Timestamp(date: domain.createdAt)

1→//
     2→//  AIChatService.swift
     3→//  FitToday
     4→//
     5→//  Wraps NewOpenAIClient for conversational chat mode (FitOrb).
     6→//
     7→
     8→import Foundation
     9→
    10→actor AIChatService {
    11→
    12→    // MARK: - Types
    13→
    14→    enum ServiceError: LocalizedError {
    15→        case clientUnavailable
    16→        case emptyResponse
    17→
    18→        var errorDescription: String? {
    19→            switch self {
    20→            case .clientUnavailable:
    21→                return "OpenAI client is not available. Please configure your API key."
    22→            case .emptyResponse:
    23→                return "Received an empty response from the assistant."
    24→            }
    25→        }
    26→    }
    27→
    28→    // MARK: - Properties
    29→
    30→    private let client: NewOpenAIClient
    31→    private let profileRepository: UserProfileRepository
    32→    private let statsRepository: UserStatsRepository
    33→    private let historyRepository: WorkoutHistoryRepository
    34→    private let promptBuilder = ChatSystemPromptBuilder()
    35→
    36→    private var cachedSystemPrompt: String?
    37→
    38→    // MARK: - Initialization
    39→
    40→    init(
    41→        client: NewOpenAIClient,
    42→        profileRepository: UserProfileRepository,
    43→        statsRepository: UserStatsRepository,
    44→        historyRepository: WorkoutHistoryRepository
    45→    ) {
    46→        self.client = client
    47→        self.profileRepository = profileRepository
    48→        self.statsRepository = statsRepository
    49→        self.historyRepository = historyRepository
    50→    }
    51→
    52→    // MARK: - Public API
    53→
    54→    /// Sends a message along with the conversation history and returns the assistant response.
    55→    func sendMessage(_ message: String, history: [AIChatMessage]) async throws -> String {
    56→        let systemPrompt = await buildContextualPrompt()
    57→
    58→        var chatMessages: [[String: String]] = [
    59→            ["role": "system", "content": systemPrompt]
    60→        ]
    61→
    62→        for msg in history where msg.role != .system {
    63→            chatMessages.append([
    64→                "role": msg.role.rawValue,
    65→                "content": msg.content
    66→            ])
    67→        }
    68→
    69→        chatMessages.append([
    70→            "role": "user",
    71→            "content": message
    72→        ])
    73→
    74→        return try await client.sendChat(messages: chatMessages, maxTokens: 1000, temperature: 0.7)
    75→    }
    76→
    77→    /// Invalidates cached system prompt (call when starting new conversation).
    78→    func invalidatePromptCache() {
    79→        cachedSystemPrompt = nil
    80→    }
    81→
    82→    // MARK: - Private
    83→
    84→    private func buildContextualPrompt() async -> String {
    85→        if let cached = cachedSystemPrompt {
    86→            return cached
    87→        }
    88→
    89→        do {
    90→            let profile = try await profileRepository.loadProfile()
    91→            let stats = try await statsRepository.getCurrentStats()
    92→            let recentWorkouts = try await historyRepository.listEntries(limit: 3, offset: 0)
    93→
    94→            let prompt = promptBuilder.buildSystemPrompt(
    95→                profile: profile,
    96→                stats: stats,
    97→                recentWorkouts: recentWorkouts
    98→            )
    99→            cachedSystemPrompt = prompt
   100→            return prompt

1→//
     2→//  TrainerChatViewModel.swift
     3→//  FitToday
     4→//
     5→//  ViewModel for trainer chat interface.
     6→//  Currently uses mock data — real chat backend to be integrated later.
     7→//
     8→
     9→import Foundation
    10→
    11→/// A single chat message between trainer and student.
    12→struct ChatMessage: Identifiable, Sendable {
    13→    let id: String
    14→    let text: String
    15→    let senderId: String
    16→    let timestamp: Date
    17→    let isFromTrainer: Bool
    18→}
    19→
    20→@MainActor
    21→@Observable final class TrainerChatViewModel {
    22→    private(set) var messages: [ChatMessage] = []
    23→    var newMessageText = ""
    24→
    25→    private let trainerId: String
    26→    private let trainerName: String
    27→
    28→    var canSend: Bool {
    29→        !newMessageText.trimmingCharacters(in: .whitespacesAndNewlines).isEmpty
    30→    }
    31→
    32→    init(trainerId: String, trainerName: String) {
    33→        self.trainerId = trainerId
    34→        self.trainerName = trainerName
    35→        loadMockMessages()
    36→    }
    37→
    38→    func sendMessage() {
    39→        guard canSend else { return }
    40→        let message = ChatMessage(
    41→            id: UUID().uuidString,
    42→            text: newMessageText.trimmingCharacters(in: .whitespacesAndNewlines),
    43→            senderId: "current_user",
    44→            timestamp: Date(),
    45→            isFromTrainer: false
    46→        )
    47→        messages.append(message)
    48→        newMessageText = ""
    49→    }
    50→
    51→    private func loadMockMessages() {
    52→        let calendar = Calendar.current
    53→        let now = Date()
    54→        messages = [
    55→            ChatMessage(id: "1", text: "trainer.chat.mock.msg1".localized, senderId: trainerId, timestamp: calendar.date(byAdding: .hour, value: -2, to: now)!, isFromTrainer: true),
    56→            ChatMessage(id: "2", text: "trainer.chat.mock.msg2".localized, senderId: "current_user", timestamp: calendar.date(byAdding: .hour, value: -1, to: now)!, isFromTrainer: false),
    57→            ChatMessage(id: "3", text: "trainer.chat.mock.msg3".localized, senderId: trainerId, timestamp: calendar.date(byAdding: .minute, value: -45, to: now)!, isFromTrainer: true),
    58→            ChatMessage(id: "4", text: "trainer.chat.mock.msg4".localized, senderId: "current_user", timestamp: calendar.date(byAdding: .minute, value: -30, to: now)!, isFromTrainer: false),
    59→            ChatMessage(id: "5", text: "trainer.chat.mock.msg5".localized, senderId: trainerId, timestamp: calendar.date(byAdding: .minute, value: -10, to: now)!, isFromTrainer: true),
    60→        ]
    61→    }
    62→}
    63→

1→//
     2→//  FeatureFlag.swift
     3→//  FitToday
     4→//
     5→//  Feature flags for remote configuration of features.
     6→//  Used to control feature rollout and A/B testing.
     7→//
     8→
     9→import Foundation
    10→
    11→// MARK: - Feature Flag Key
    12→
    13→/// Keys for remote feature flags controlled via Firebase Remote Config.
    14→/// Each key corresponds to a boolean flag that can be toggled remotely.
    15→enum FeatureFlagKey: String, CaseIterable, Sendable {
    16→
    17→    // MARK: - AI Features
    18→
    19→    /// Enables AI-powered workout generation (OpenAI).
    20→    case aiWorkoutGenerationEnabled = "ai_workout_generation_enabled"
    21→
    22→    /// Enables AI exercise substitution during active workout.
    23→    case aiExerciseSubstitutionEnabled = "ai_exercise_substitution_enabled"
    24→
    25→    // MARK: - Social Features
    26→
    27→    /// Enables group creation, joining, and management.
    28→    case socialGroupsEnabled = "social_groups_enabled"
    29→
    30→    /// Enables workout challenges within groups.
    31→    case challengesEnabled = "challenges_enabled"
    32→
    33→    /// Enables leaderboard ranking in groups.
    34→    case leaderboardEnabled = "leaderboard_enabled"
    35→
    36→    /// Enables photo check-in for challenge validation.
    37→    case checkInEnabled = "check_in_enabled"
    38→
    39→    /// Enables group streak tracking and display.
    40→    case groupStreaksEnabled = "group_streaks_enabled"
    41→
    42→    // MARK: - Health & Sync
    43→
    44→    /// Enables Apple Health read/write integration.
    45→    case healthKitSyncEnabled = "healthkit_sync_enabled"
    46→
    47→    /// Enables activity stats with Swift Charts.
    48→    case statsChartsEnabled = "stats_charts_enabled"
    49→
    50→    // MARK: - Content
    51→
    52→    /// Enables structured training programs.
    53→    case programsEnabled = "programs_enabled"
    54→
    55→    /// Enables user-created workout templates.
    56→    case customWorkoutsEnabled = "custom_workouts_enabled"
    57→
    58→    /// Enables exercise library/explorer.
    59→    case exerciseLibraryEnabled = "exercise_library_enabled"
    60→
    61→    // MARK: - Personal Trainer
    62→
    63→    /// Enables personal trainer integration features.
    64→    /// When enabled, users can connect with personal trainers via CMS.
    65→    case personalTrainerEnabled = "personal_trainer_enabled"
    66→
    67→    /// Enables synchronization of workouts from CMS.
    68→    /// When enabled, trainer-assigned workouts sync to the user's app.
    69→    case cmsWorkoutSyncEnabled = "cms_workout_sync_enabled"
    70→
    71→    /// Enables chat functionality with personal trainers.
    72→    /// When enabled, users can message their assigned trainer.
    73→    case trainerChatEnabled = "trainer_chat_enabled"
    74→
    75→    // MARK: - Monetization & UX
    76→
    77→    /// Enables paywall/Pro upgrade prompts.
    78→    case paywallEnabled = "paywall_enabled"
    79→
    80→    /// Enables App Store review request after workout completion.
    81→    case reviewRequestEnabled = "review_request_enabled"
    82→
    83→    // MARK: - Operational
    84→
    85→    /// Shows maintenance banner and disables write operations.
    86→    case maintenanceModeEnabled = "maintenance_mode_enabled"
    87→
    88→    /// Shows non-dismissable update dialog directing to App Store.
    89→    case forceUpdateEnabled = "force_update_enabled"
    90→
    91→    // MARK: - Display Name
    92→
    93→    /// Human-readable display name for the feature flag.
    94→    var displayName: String {
    95→        switch self {
    96→        // AI
    97→        case .aiWorkoutGenerationEnabled: return "AI Workout Generation"
    98→        case .aiExerciseSubstitutionEnabled: return "AI Exercise Substitution"
    99→        // Social
   100→        case .socialGroupsEnabled: return "Social Groups"
   101→        case .challengesEnabled: return "Challenges"
   102→        case .leaderboardEnabled: return "Leaderboard"
   103→        case .checkInEnabled: return "Check-In"
   104→        case .groupStreaksEnabled: return "Group Streaks"
   105→        // Health
   106→        case .healthKitSyncEnabled: return "Apple Health Sync"
   107→        case .statsChartsEnabled: return "Stats Charts"
   108→        // Content
   109→        case .programsEnabled: return "Training Programs"
   110→        case .customWorkoutsEnabled: return "Custom Workouts"
   111→        case .exerciseLibraryEnabled: return "Exercise Library"
   112→        // Personal Trainer
   113→        case .personalTrainerEnabled: return "Personal Trainer Integration"
   114→        case .cmsWorkoutSyncEnabled: return "CMS Workout Sync"
   115→        case .trainerChatEnabled: return "Trainer Chat"
   116→        // Monetization
   117→        case .paywallEnabled: return "Paywall"
   118→        case .reviewRequestEnabled: return "Review Request"
   119→        // Operational
   120→        case .maintenanceModeEnabled: return "Maintenance Mode"
   121→        case .forceUpdateEnabled: return "Force Update"
   122→        }
   123→    }
   124→
   125→    // MARK: - Default Value
   126→
   127→    /// Default value when remote config is unavailable.
   128→    /// Existing features default to `true` (no regression).
   129→    /// Unreleased features default to `false`.
   130→    var defaultValue: Bool {
   131→        switch self {
   132→        // Unreleased features — disabled by default
   133→        case .personalTrainerEnabled,
   134→             .cmsWorkoutSyncEnabled,
   135→             .trainerChatEnabled,
   136→             .maintenanceModeEnabled,
   137→             .forceUpdateEnabled:
   138→            return false
   139→
   140→        // All existing features — enabled by default
   141→        default:
   142→            return true
   143→        }
   144→    }
   145→}
   146→

1→//
     2→//  RemoteConfigService.swift
     3→//  FitToday
     4→//
     5→//  Service that wraps Firebase Remote Config for feature flag management.
     6→//  Uses actor isolation for thread-safe access to remote config values.
     7→//
     8→
     9→import Foundation
    10→import FirebaseRemoteConfig
    11→
    12→// MARK: - Remote Config Service Protocol
    13→
    14→/// Protocol for remote configuration service.
    15→protocol RemoteConfigServicing: Sendable {
    16→    /// Fetches and activates remote config values from Firebase.
    17→    /// - Throws: Error if fetch or activation fails.
    18→    func fetchAndActivate() async throws
    19→
    20→    /// Gets the boolean value for a feature flag key.
    21→    /// - Parameter key: The feature flag key to retrieve.
    22→    /// - Returns: The boolean value for the key, or the default value if not set.
    23→    func getValue(for key: FeatureFlagKey) async -> Bool
    24→
    25→    /// Sets the minimum fetch interval for remote config.
    26→    /// - Parameter interval: The minimum interval between fetches.
    27→    func setMinimumFetchInterval(_ interval: TimeInterval) async
    28→}
    29→
    30→// MARK: - Remote Config Service
    31→
    32→/// Actor that wraps Firebase Remote Config for thread-safe feature flag access.
    33→/// Manages fetching, caching, and retrieving remote configuration values.
    34→actor RemoteConfigService: RemoteConfigServicing {
    35→    // MARK: - Properties
    36→
    37→    private let remoteConfig: RemoteConfig
    38→    private var lastFetchTime: Date?
    39→    private var isConfigured = false
    40→
    41→    /// Default fetch interval: 12 hours for production.
    42→    static let defaultFetchInterval: TimeInterval = 12 * 60 * 60
    43→
    44→    /// Debug fetch interval: 0 seconds for immediate updates.
    45→    static let debugFetchInterval: TimeInterval = 0
    46→
    47→    // MARK: - Initialization
    48→
    49→    init(remoteConfig: RemoteConfig = RemoteConfig.remoteConfig()) {
    50→        self.remoteConfig = remoteConfig
    51→        configureDefaults()
    52→    }
    53→
    54→    // MARK: - Configuration
    55→
    56→    /// Configures default values for all feature flags.
    57→    private func configureDefaults() {
    58→        guard !isConfigured else { return }
    59→
    60→        // Build default values dictionary
    61→        var defaults: [String: NSObject] = [:]
    62→        for key in FeatureFlagKey.allCases {
    63→            defaults[key.rawValue] = NSNumber(value: key.defaultValue)
    64→        }
    65→
    66→        remoteConfig.setDefaults(defaults)
    67→
    68→        // Configure settings
    69→        let settings = RemoteConfigSettings()
    70→        #if DEBUG
    71→        settings.minimumFetchInterval = Self.debugFetchInterval
    72→        #else
    73→        settings.minimumFetchInterval = Self.defaultFetchInterval
    74→        #endif
    75→
    76→        remoteConfig.configSettings = settings
    77→        isConfigured = true
    78→    }
    79→
    80→    // MARK: - Public Methods
    81→
    82→    func fetchAndActivate() async throws {
    83→        do {
    84→            let status = try await remoteConfig.fetchAndActivate()
    85→
    86→            switch status {
    87→            case .successFetchedFromRemote:
    88→                lastFetchTime = Date()
    89→                #if DEBUG
    90→                print("[RemoteConfig] Successfully fetched from remote")
    91→                #endif
    92→
    93→            case .successUsingPreFetchedData:
    94→                #if DEBUG
    95→                print("[RemoteConfig] Using pre-fetched data")
    96→                #endif
    97→
    98→            case .error:
    99→                #if DEBUG
   100→                print("[RemoteConfig] Fetch returned error status")
   101→                #endif
   102→
   103→            @unknown default:
   104→                #if DEBUG
   105→                print("[RemoteConfig] Unknown fetch status")
   106→                #endif
   107→            }
   108→        } catch {
   109→            #if DEBUG
   110→            print("[RemoteConfig] Fetch failed: \(error.localizedDescription)")
   111→            #endif
   112→            throw RemoteConfigError.fetchFailed(underlying: error)
   113→        }
   114→    }
   115→
   116→    func getValue(for key: FeatureFlagKey) async -> Bool {
   117→        let configValue = remoteConfig.configValue(forKey: key.rawValue)
   118→
   119→        // If no value is set remotely, use default
   120→        if configValue.source == .static {
   121→            return key.defaultValue
   122→        }
   123→
   124→        return configValue.boolValue
   125→    }
   126→
   127→    func setMinimumFetchInterval(_ interval: TimeInterval) async {
   128→        let settings = RemoteConfigSettings()
   129→        settings.minimumFetchInterval = interval
   130→        remoteConfig.configSettings = settings
   131→    }
   132→
   133→    // MARK: - Debug Helpers
   134→
   135→    #if DEBUG
   136→    /// Returns the source of a config value (for debugging).
   137→    func getValueSource(for key: FeatureFlagKey) async -> RemoteConfigSource {
   138→        remoteConfig.configValue(forKey: key.rawValue).source
   139→    }
   140→
   141→    /// Returns all current config values (for debugging).
   142→    func getAllValues() async -> [String: Bool] {
   143→        var values: [String: Bool] = [:]
   144→        for key in FeatureFlagKey.allCases {
   145→            values[key.rawValue] = await getValue(for: key)
   146→        }
   147→        return values
   148→    }
   149→    #endif
   150→}
   151→
   152→// MARK: - Remote Config Error
   153→
   154→/// Errors that can occur during remote config operations.
   155→enum RemoteConfigError: LocalizedError {
   156→    case fetchFailed(underlying: Error)
   157→    case activationFailed(underlying: Error)
   158→    case notConfigured
   159→
   160→    var errorDescription: String? {
   161→        switch self {
   162→        case .fetchFailed(let error):
   163→            return "Failed to fetch remote config: \(error.localizedDescription)"
   164→        case .activationFailed(let error):
   165→            return "Failed to activate remote config: \(error.localizedDescription)"
   166→        case .notConfigured:
   167→            return "Remote config service is not configured"
   168→        }
   169→    }
   170→}
   171→

     8→import Foundation
     9→
    10→// NOTE: Add FirebaseAnalytics to your SPM dependencies:
    11→// Package: https://github.com/firebase/firebase-ios-sdk.git
    12→// Product: FirebaseAnalytics
    13→#if canImport(FirebaseAnalytics)
    14→import FirebaseAnalytics
    15→#endif
    16→
    17→// MARK: - AnalyticsService Protocol
    18→
    19→/// Protocol for tracking analytics events. Allows for testing and alternative implementations.
    20→protocol AnalyticsTracking: Sendable {
    21→    func trackGroupCreated(groupId: String, userId: String)
    22→    func trackGroupJoined(groupId: String, userId: String, inviteSource: InviteSource)
    23→    func trackWorkoutSynced(userId: String, groupId: String, challengeType: ChallengeType, value: Int)
    24→    func trackGroupLeft(groupId: String, userId: String, durationDays: Int)
    25→    func setUserInGroup(_ isInGroup: Bool)
    26→    func setUserRole(_ role: GroupRole?)
    27→}
    28→
    29→// MARK: - InviteSource
    30→
    31→enum InviteSource: String, Sendable {
    32→    case link
    33→    case qr
    34→}
    35→
    36→// MARK: - FirebaseAnalyticsService
    37→
    38→/// Firebase Analytics implementation for tracking user events.
    39→final class FirebaseAnalyticsService: AnalyticsTracking, @unchecked Sendable {
    40→
    41→    // MARK: - Event Names
    42→
    43→    private enum EventName {
    44→        static let groupCreated = "group_created"
    45→        static let groupJoined = "group_joined"
    46→        static let workoutSynced = "workout_synced"
    47→        static let groupLeft = "group_left"
    48→    }
    49→
    50→    // MARK: - Parameter Keys
    51→
    52→    private enum ParameterKey {
    53→        static let groupId = "group_id"
    54→        static let userId = "user_id"
    55→        static let inviteSource = "invite_source"
    56→        static let challengeType = "challenge_type"
    57→        static let value = "value"
    58→        static let durationDays = "duration_days"
    59→        static let timestamp = "timestamp"
    60→    }
    61→
    62→    // MARK: - User Property Keys
    63→
    64→    private enum UserPropertyKey {
    65→        static let isInGroup = "is_in_group"
    66→        static let groupRole = "group_role"
    67→    }
    68→
    69→    // MARK: - Event Tracking
    70→
